items.title: Newell's Old Boys werkt aan romantische terugkeer Messi
items.summary: Komt het dan toch nog van een romantische terugkeer op het oude nest voor Lionel Messi? Als het aan Newell's Old Boys ligt, wel. Vicevoorzitter Juan Manuel Medina heeft bevestigd dat de Argentijnse club bezig is om de 38-jarige legende volgend jaar terug te halen.

items.title: Kanté tóch naar Fenerbahçe: En-Nesyri tekent in Saoedi-Arabië
items.summary: N'Golo Kanté is terug op de Europese velden. De 34-jarige controleur heeft een contract getekend bij Fenerbahçe, nadat Al-Ittihad zijn verbintenis dinsdagavond heeft ontbonden. Youssef En-Nesyri vertrekt juist naar Saoedi-Arabië.

items.title: Arsenal see off Chelsea to reach Carabao Cup final
items.summary: Arsenal reached their first major final in six years after a 1-0 win over Chelsea at the Emirates Stadium on Tuesday saw them progress to the final of the Carabao Cup 4-2 on aggregate.

items.title: Barça survive Copa scare by giant-killers Albacete
items.summary: Barcelona reached the semifinals of the Copa del Rey, but only after making hard work of beating giantkillers Albacete 2-1.

items.title: Source: Minnesota in talks with James Rodríguez
items.summary: Minnesota United is in discussions with Colombia international star forward James Rodríguez, a source confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Pep: Pressure on City's big-spending PL rivals
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has said the pressure is on six of Manchester City's Premier League rivals to win more trophies because they have had a higher net spend.

items.title: Fener blame Al Ittihad for Kante transfer fiasco
items.summary: N'Golo Kanté's switch from Al Ittihad to Fenerbahce has collapsed, the day after the transfer window closed.

items.title: Deadline Day: Serie B-club wil topscorer FC Den Bosch, ruildeal Telstar en Sparta
items.summary: Het is Deadline Day in Nederland, voor de VriendenLoterij Eredivisie en Keuken Kampioen Divisie. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers op de laatste transferdag van Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland , waar het gisteren Deadline Day was.

items.title: FC Den Bosch wijst Italiaans lastminutebod op Monzialo af
items.summary: FC Den Bosch heeft op de valreep van de transferwindow nog een bod op Kévin Monzialo afgewezen. De spits kon een transfer maken naar US Catanzaro. Dat bevestigen ingewijden.

items.title: Lammers ontgoocheld na uitschakeling: 'Leek wel een schiettent'
items.summary: FC Twente produceerde maar liefst 36 doelpogingen in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker, maar uiteindelijk was het AZ dat na verlenging in eigen huis wist te winnen en doorstootte naar de halve finale. Na afloop spraken we met spits Sam Lammers, die diep teleurgesteld was na de bekeruitschakeling.

items.title: Kai Havertz adds gloss as Arsenal dispatch Chelsea to book Carabao Cup final spot
items.summary: When the final whistle blew, you could sense the huge wave of relief that swept over this stadium. Arsenal may not have been at all convincing against a Chelsea side who caused them the occasional problem but Mikel Arteta will not care one bit. After four successive semi-final defeats, Arsenal are on their way to Wembley at last. After the drama of the first leg at Stamford Bridge that saw Liam Rosenior’s side travel to north London needing to overturn a 3-2 deficit, this was a much more cagey affair that was eventually settled in injury time by substitute Kai Havertz against his former club. Continue reading...

items.title: Havertz strikes as Arsenal beat Chelsea to reach Carabao Cup final
items.summary: Arsenal claimed their place in the Carabao Cup final as Kai Havertz's breakaway goal in stoppage time sealed a 1-0 win over Chelsea at the Emirates Stadium and a 4-2 victory on aggregate.

items.title: 'The ultimate climax!' | Havertz scores last-gasp winner for Arsenal against Chelsea
items.summary: Kai Havertz scored in the final seconds of the second leg of the Carabao Cup semi-final against his former club Chelsea, to ensure Arsenal progress to the final at Wembley.

items.title: 'He's a work in progress off the field' - McCullum defends 'sensational' Brook
items.summary: England head coach Brendon McCullum has praised the way Harry Brook has dealt with recent controversy but admits his white-ball captain is a "work in progress" away from the cricket field.

items.title: Sheff Utd win; Hull miss chance & Blackburn out of drop zone - EFL highlights
St Mirren 1-0 Hearts highlights
Has the January transfer window always felt flat?
items.summary: With just seven Premier League deals done on transfer deadline day, has the winter transfer window become a damp squib?

items.title: Cristiano Ronaldo’s no-show leaves Saudi Pro League facing awkward questions
items.summary: League’s star said to be unhappy over his club’s transfer dealings and Karim Benzema’s move. Now he could face his first backlash Jurassic Park sounded great given the spectacular beasts on display, but there was chaos after they started to do their own thing. When Cristiano Ronaldo, surely the T rex, and Karim Benzema, perhaps a velociraptor, are scoring in spectacular fashion there are headlines around the world, but the Saudi Pro League is finding out that when they start to flex their muscles off the pitch, there is even more interest and, it turns out, a real problem for the competition. What happened on a manic Monday in the SPL should have been about what unfolded on the pitch. Al-Hilal, in first, drew with third-placed Al-Ahli. Al-Nassr won, to stay second, closing to within a point of the leaders. If Brendan Rodgers, having a whale of a time with Al-Qadsiah, wins his game in hand then four points will separate the top four with just over a third of the season remaining. It is the kind of title race most leagues would love. Continue reading...

items.title: Lens condemn racist abuse of Saint-Maximin after player’s children targeted in Mexico
items.summary: Winger suffers online racism on return to France Abuse of Frenchman’s family led him to leave Mexico Lens have condemned the racist abuse aimed at new signing Allan Saint-Maximin after a previous racist incident involving the former Newcastle winger’s children ended his career in Mexico’s top flight. Saint-Maximin joined Lens on a six-month deal during the winter transfer window. He left Mexican side Club América, saying his children were the victims of racist abuse there. Continue reading...

items.title: Football Daily | Naughty objects and Odemwingie: deadline day used to be box office – not any more
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! This used to be a country where transfer deadline day meant something. Gone are the days that Football Daily could buy a Freddo for 10p and gone are the days that Sky Sports news reporters could be found lurking in the winter rain outside Premier League training grounds at 11pm (GMT), decked in their finest yellow ties and fighting for their broadcasting lives against youths brandishing naughty purple objects as they brought news of Giannelli Imbula’s impending club-record move from Porto to Stoke City. From dodgy fax machines to Jim White, to Peter Odenwingie in a car park, to Alfredo Di Stéfano’s dubious expression towards Julien Faubert [and don’t forget roll-up man – Football Daily Ed], deadline day used to be box office, worth staying up past your bedtime for. Interesting idea from Yannick Woudstra in yesterday’s Football Daily letters. But as a way to help Spurs win games, let me be one of the 1,057 to point out that it will surely depend on which 45 minutes they turn up to play in?” – Simon Mazier. What a coincidence that Yannick is suggesting that games be reduced to 45 minutes, the same day that Jonathan Wilson writes that if that were so, Manchester City would be top of the league by 12 points. I never thought that the league ought to split into first and second half divisions. Maybe the title could be played out with the winners of both divisions” – Nigel Sanders. If Snoop Dogg (yesterday’s Next Episode section, full email edition) reckons that the Swans have a shout of making the playoffs, someone should check if he’s smoking something. Just say no, kids” – Martin Clifford. Continue reading...
